Root Cause: Packaging design deficiency - inadequate product restraint within packaging allowing movement, resulting in surface damage and scratching
Corrective Action: Two-stage packaging redesign: Phase 1 (Oct 2025) - replaced EPE foam with honeycomb structure to improve product restraint. Phase 2 - implemented leg immobilization features to prevent component movement and eliminate scratch defects.
Notes: Phase 1 implementation reduced defect rate from 3 units/day to 1 unit/week. Phase 2 packaging reinforcement addresses residual minor scratch occurrences.

Root Cause: Inadequate incoming material inspection at supplier stage and insufficient in-process inspection at GL factory. Contributing factor: packaging design allowing product movement.
Corrective Action: Implemented 100% visual inspection protocol at supplier and GL facility. Expected gradual defect rate reduction as process control matures.
Notes: KFT incoming QC inspection gate for Jasper shelf components demonstrating significant reduction in inspection failure rate. Overall Radaro quality incidents attributed to Jasper shelf finish defects showing measurable improvement.

Root Cause: Warehouse inventory control deficiency - damaged legacy stock remains in active inventory without segregation or dispositioning
Corrective Action: Supplier packaging redesign implemented for new production. Warehouse stock requires 100% inspection with non-conforming inventory disposition to clearance channel rather than customer shipment. Issue escalated to Shane and Tony for warehouse inventory dispositioning.
Notes: Warehouse inventory requires inspection audit with damaged stock removed from customer-available inventory and redirected to clearance disposition

Root Cause: Inspection process non-conformance - multiple products inspected against obsolete quality standards. Traceability system deficiency - no supplier-level defect attribution capability.
Material handling equipment degradation - corrosion transfer from rusted storage trolleys to sofa frame assemblies during in-process handling at GL facility
Corrective Action: All material handling trolleys upgraded with hot-dip galvanized surface treatment to eliminate corrosion transfer risk. Established visual inspection gates post-welding and at assembly stage.
Notes: Process controls expected to substantially reduce corrosion-related defects

Root Cause: Design deficiency - bracket and fastening connection insufficient to withstand anticipated load conditions during backrest use
Corrective Action: Engineering change implemented - backrest bracket redesigned with increased load-bearing capacity and reinforced connection method

Root Cause: Component specification inadequacy - hinge load capacity insufficient to support door weight, resulting in misalignment during transit and installation
Corrective Action: Component upgrade implemented - replaced hinges with higher load-bearing specification across all door products. Validation testing completed. Note: Smithfield warehouse contains legacy inventory with previous specification. New specification applied to all Dune product line.
Notes: Implemented for all new production batches and new product lines

Root Cause: Component design deficiency - knocking retention mechanism inadequate to withstand handling forces during delivery operations and seat frame removal. Multiple field failures reported by delivery personnel and customers indicating systematic design inadequacy.
Corrective Action: Engineering change implemented - knocking retention mechanism redesigned. Validation testing completed through multiple seat removal and reinstallation cycles confirming adequate strength and retention under operational conditions.

Root Cause: Design deficiency - leg tube specification lacks drain hole, causing E-coat process liquid entrapment and subsequent leakage post-coating
Corrective Action: Engineering drawing revision issued incorporating drain hole feature. Established post-coating inspection checkpoint - QC inspector verifies drain hole presence and liquid evacuation compliance.

Root Cause: ERP/planning system deficiency resulting in component allocation non-conformance - leg components allocated to different manufacturing facility than corresponding frame assemblies
Corrective Action: Implemented 3-point verification checkpoint at master planning stage. Established secondary verification gate at GL factory planning level. Deployed operator-level and in-process QC inspection specific to leg component matching at China facility.
Notes: Multi-stage process controls expected to significantly reduce missing component non-conformances over subsequent months

Root Cause: Packaging design deficiency - EPE foam cushioning inadequate structural strength, loose packaging configuration allowing product movement resulting in transit damage
Corrective Action: Packaging redesign implemented - multi-layer protection system utilizing EPE foam + rigid board frame + exterior carton for enhanced structural integrity and product restraint

Root Cause: Equipment contamination - hydraulic oil leakage from turret mast causing product surface contamination during material handling operations
Corrective Action: Containment action: staging area 100% inspection protocol initiated to identify contaminated units. Non-conforming product (damaged bags, scuff marks, oil contamination) segregated to refurbishment for cleaning and repackaging. Preventive measure: turret equipment maintenance to identify and eliminate oil leakage source.

Product:
Cushions in Non-Woven Bag
Top Contributor of Fabric Issues
Root Cause: Packaging material deficiency - 70GSM non-woven protective bags inadequate to prevent contamination transfer to cushion covers during warehouse handling, staging, and delivery operations. Average defect rate: 3 units/day. Issue escalated to Ben & Miao 18/11/2025. Contributing factor: inadequate material handling practices at warehouse and delivery stages resulting in increased cost of quality (rework/cleaning) and production capacity loss (remakes).
Corrective Action: Two-phase CAPA: (1) Packaging specification revision - increase non-woven bag material from 70GSM to 150GSM. (2) Establish improved material handling protocols for warehouse operations and delivery personnel.
Notes: Packaging material upgrade will provide containment action; however, sustained defect reduction requires implementation of improved handling procedures by warehouse and delivery operations.

Root Cause: Equipment maintenance deficiency - conveyor bridge components in degraded condition causing contact marking on product during in-process transport
Corrective Action: Conveyor bridge configuration redesigned and implemented. In-process QC monitoring established to verify no contact marking occurs during transport.
Notes: Equipment modification provides preventive measure. Manufacturing Engineering team established preventive maintenance schedule to sustain equipment condition.

Root Cause: Process non-conformance - loading team using red marker directly on non-woven protective bags causing ink bleed-through and contamination transfer to cushion surfaces
Corrective Action: Immediate containment action: red marker usage discontinued. Revised work instruction: marking permitted on labels only. Random inspection audit protocol established to verify compliance.
Notes: Factory implementing ongoing compliance audits with permanent red marker elimination

Root Cause: Product getting dirty in stagging and on delivery
Corrective Action: Stagging area is being checked, and drivers are constantly feed back to have cleaning kits and clean at the time of delivery
